channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: 'It's absolutely exciting!': Astronomers discover an atmosphere around an Earth-like planet in the habitable zone for first time ever - Live Science
item.description:
1. 'It's absolutely exciting!': Astronomers discover an atmosphere around an Earth-like planet in the habitable zone for first time ever  Live Science
  2. Astronomers Find an Atmosphere on a Nearby Earthlike Planet  The New York Times
  3. Earth-like exoplanet found to have an atmosphere  The Guardian
  4. Scientists Discover a Planet With the Most Earth-like Conditions Yet  Time Magazine
  5. First Atmosphere Detected on a Terrestrial World  Sky & Telescope

item.title: NASA's Psyche mission delivers Mars flyby data, time-lapse video - Phys.org
item.description:
1. NASA's Psyche mission delivers Mars flyby data, time-lapse video  Phys.org
  2. NASA has just released a jaw-dropping video of a spacecraft's view as it zoomed past Mars. You've got to see this  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  3. The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches  NASA Science (.gov)
  4. NASA’s Psyche Spacecraft Captures Mind-Bending Month-Long Timelapse of Mars  PetaPixel
  5. DTU Instrument Clears First Test for Psyche Mission  Mirage News

item.title: Cells from your mother likely infiltrated your brain in the womb, and they could survive for decades, study reveals - Live Science
item.description: Cells from your mother likely infiltrated your brain in the womb, and they could survive for decades, study reveals  Live Science

item.description: A teen spotted a rare pair of birds in D.C. Then came another surprise.  The Washington Post

item.title: Underwater drones discover coral reefs off Benin, long presumed dead, teeming with life - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Underwater drones discover coral reefs off Benin, long presumed dead, teeming with life  Phys.org
  2. The Thriving Coral Reef that ‘Defeated Time’ in West Africa  Inside Climate News
  3. Image 2 of the reef  EurekAlert!
  4. “It Was a Mix of Excitement and Disbelief”: When Scientists Sent Deep-Sea Rovers to Confirm a 1960s Theory, They Made a Surprising Discovery  The Debrief
  5. Coral reef long presumed dead rediscovered "teeming with life" off coast of Africa  Yahoo

item.title: An asteroid shattered near Jupiter’s gravitational gateway 800 million years ago; scientists say the debr - The Times of India
item.description:
1. An asteroid shattered near Jupiter’s gravitational gateway 800 million years ago; scientists say the debr  The Times of India
  2. Ancient Asteroid Shattered Near Jupiter's Gravity Trap, Bombarded Earth for 150 Million Years  Tech Times

  4. One cause for “Snowball Earth”? Asteroid collision with dramatic consequences  heise online
  5. A shattered asteroid may have bombarded Earth 800 million years ago  ScienceDaily

item.title: The Perseid meteor shower is here and skywatchers could see up to 100 shooting stars per hour - FOX Weather
item.description:

  4. Biggest meteor shower of the year is here. How long Perseid will last  The Asheville Citizen Times

item.title: Spending more time in bright daylight could transform your sleep, according to new study - New York Post
item.description:
1. Spending more time in bright daylight could transform your sleep, according to new study  New York Post

  4. Brighter Days Linked To Better Sleep, Study Finds  Mirage News
  5. The daytime habit that could transform your sleep, according to new study  AOL.com

item.title: A US tech start-up wants to turn night into day. The question is why? - ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
item.description:
1. A US tech start-up wants to turn night into day. The question is why?  ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
  2. U.S. approval of a controversial satellite has scientists asking: Who’s really in charge of space?  Scientific American
  3. Opinion | Goodbye Darkness, My Old Friend  WSJ
  4. Why a Single Sun‑Reflecting Satellite Has Scientists Sounding the Alarm  Yahoo
  5. Observers Beware: Reflect Orbital’s Space Mirrors Approved for Launch  Sky & Telescope
item.source: ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation

item.title: A Quebecer noticed a strange circular pit on Google Maps while planning a trip; scientists have now confi - The Times of India
item.description:
1. A Quebecer noticed a strange circular pit on Google Maps while planning a trip; scientists have now confi  The Times of India
  2. Pit found by Quebecer confirmed to be a 390-million-years-old meteorite impact crater  CTV News
  3. Giant pit discovered by hiker on Google Maps confirmed to be 390 million-year-old meteor impact crater  Live Science
  4. He saw a pit on Google Maps. It turned out to be a 390-million-year-old meteor crater  CBC
  5. Hidden In Plain View? Man Discovers Giant Meteor Crater Older Than Dinosaurs On Google Maps  News18

item.title: We’re Underestimating the Power of ‘One-in-a-Thousand-Year’ Solar Storms, NASA Warns - Gizmodo
item.description:
1. We’re Underestimating the Power of ‘One-in-a-Thousand-Year’ Solar Storms, NASA Warns  Gizmodo

  4. New NASA Study Says Possibly No Limit to Solar Storm Effects  NASA Science (.gov)
  5. Power grids face unexpected slow-burn threat from solar storms  The Hindu

item.title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers how black holes feed themselves - Space
item.description:
1. James Webb Space Telescope discovers how black holes feed themselves  Space

  3. UK researcher helps solve longstanding black hole mystery  UKNow
  4. Black holes survive on a ‘feedback loop’. NASA’s space telescope has proof  ThePrint
  5. James Webb Telescope reveals clue about supermassive black holes  Minute Mirror

item.title: A Major Ocean Current Is On The Verge of Collapse. Scientists Say The Effects May Reach California - ScienceAlert
item.description:

  4. After record heat, could the Atlantic make Britain's weather even more extreme?  BBC
  5. In 10-20 Years, Major Climate-Regulating Ocean Current Could Degrade, Threatening Planet's Future  ThePetitionSite.com

item.description: Flying lab maps Utah’s ozone problem in hopes of finding a solution  Deseret News

item.title: We assume life needs a sun, but a team at Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich calculated in 2026 that moons orbiting starless rogue planets could sustain liquid oceans for up to 4.3 billion years — roughly as long as Earth has existed — without a single ra - Space Daily
item.description: We assume life needs a sun, but a team at Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich calculated in 2026 that moons orbiting starless rogue planets could sustain liquid oceans for up to 4.3 billion years — roughly as long as Earth has existed — without a single ra  Space Daily

item.title: Scientists identify the rare meteorite that killed the dinosaurs 66 million years ago - ScienceDaily
item.description:
1. Scientists identify the rare meteorite that killed the dinosaurs 66 million years ago  ScienceDaily

  4. The Dinosaur Extinction Was More Complicated Than You Think  New Scientist

item.title: Scientists told them, 'No, it's too dangerous,' but they did it anyway: Inside Japan's super-close asteroid... - Yahoo

item.title: The mysterious hum heard around the world may finally have an explanation - ScienceDaily
item.description:

item.title: 10 places in the US and Canada to see August's partial solar eclipse and Perseid meteor shower in 1 trip - Space
item.description:

  4. A solar eclipse is coming to Europe this summer  The Washington Post
  5. Total solar eclipse drives tourist bookings across Tarragona, Ebre region and Lleida  Catalan News

item.title: In 1970, Venera 7 appeared to fall silent when it hit Venus, but engineers later found 23 minutes of faint radio data buried in the recording — the first transmission ever received from the surface of another planet - Space Daily
item.description: In 1970, Venera 7 appeared to fall silent when it hit Venus, but engineers later found 23 minutes of faint radio data buried in the recording — the first transmission ever received from the surface of another planet  Space Daily

item.title: Scientists used children’s ‘silly sprinklers’ to solve a physics problem Richard Feynman could not; the r - The Times of India
item.description:
1. Scientists used children’s ‘silly sprinklers’ to solve a physics problem Richard Feynman could not; the r  The Times of India
  2. Physicists Have Argued Over This Problem Since 1883. It May Now Be Solved.  The New York Times
  3. “Silly sprinklers” help scientists finally solve Feynman’s famous sprinkler mystery  ScienceDaily
  4. Feynman Sprinkler Problem Solved: Momentum Flux, Not Fluid Swirl, Spins Both Ways  Tech Times
  5. Mines researcher helps put “silly sprinklers” in reverse to unravel decades-old physics puzzle  Mines Newsroom

item.title: The First Room-Temperature Quantum Material of Its Kind Is Spun From Atoms of Gold - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. The First Room-Temperature Quantum Material of Its Kind Is Spun From Atoms of Gold  ScienceAlert

  3. LSU physicists create first room-temperature quantum material  Louisiana State University
  4. Gold Nanoantennas Sort Quantum Light Like a Semiconductor Sorts Electrons  Tech Times
  5. First Successful Development of Room-Temperature Quantum Materials: A Major Leap for Quantum Computers and Solar Cells?  XenoSpectrum

item.title: When Spirit’s front wheel failed on Mars, engineers drove the rover backwards and let the dead wheel scrape through the soil — accidentally uncovering bright silica that pointed to ancient hot springs or steam vents - Space Daily
item.description: When Spirit’s front wheel failed on Mars, engineers drove the rover backwards and let the dead wheel scrape through the soil — accidentally uncovering bright silica that pointed to ancient hot springs or steam vents  Space Daily

  3. Time stops along with chaos in the quantum Universe  Universe Space Tech

item.title: Astronauts who have seen the whole Earth at once describe the overview effect — every war and border and argument hanging in silence — and afterwards traffic and small talk can feel strangely far away, like the world seen through glass - Space Daily
item.description: Astronauts who have seen the whole Earth at once describe the overview effect — every war and border and argument hanging in silence — and afterwards traffic and small talk can feel strangely far away, like the world seen through glass  Space Daily

item.title: China plans to hit an asteroid with a Mach 26 projectile in planet defence test - South China Morning Post
item.description:
1. China plans to hit an asteroid with a Mach 26 projectile in planet defence test  South China Morning Post
  2. China plans bold asteroid collision test to outpace NASA's DART mission  Interesting Engineering
  3. Why is China planning to crash a spacecraft into an asteroid at 9 km/sec? | A second aircraft will observe the asteroid | Inshorts  Inshorts
  4. China is building an asteroid monitoring network that will use telescopes and satellites for planetary defence: Here’s how it will work  The Times of India
  5. China eyes first kinetic impact test for asteroid defense, targeting smaller and less predictable asteroids than NASA’s DART  Global Times

item.title: Is Working Memory the Subconscious Engine of Consciousness? - Neuroscience News
item.description: Is Working Memory the Subconscious Engine of Consciousness?  Neuroscience News

item.title: Cassini watched bright “islands” appear, disappear and reappear in Titan’s methane seas — and laboratory experiments suggest they may be fields of nitrogen bubbles fizzing out of solution as the alien lakes warm or mix - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Cassini watched bright “islands” appear, disappear and reappear in Titan’s methane seas — and laboratory experiments suggest they may be fields of nitrogen bubbles fizzing out of solution as the alien lakes warm or mix  Space Daily
  2. Saturn's moon Titan runs the same weather cycle as Earth — clouds, rain, rivers and seas — except the liquid doing all the work is methane, and the bedrock underfoot is water frozen at nearly minus 180 degrees, harder than most stone on Earth  Space Daily
  3. Saturn’s Largest Moon May Hold the Resources for a Space Colony  SciTechDaily
  4. Titan could be humanity’s next deep space destination  A News
  5. Titan is the only place beyond Earth where a spacecraft has landed on a surface with liquid nearby, and the probe sent back the sound of alien wind before falling silent forever  Space Daily

item.title: Across Titan’s equator, winds have built dunes around 100 metres high and hundreds of kilometres long — but the grains are water ice coated in hydrocarbons that fell out of the moon’s orange atmosphere - Space Daily
item.description: Across Titan’s equator, winds have built dunes around 100 metres high and hundreds of kilometres long — but the grains are water ice coated in hydrocarbons that fell out of the moon’s orange atmosphere  Space Daily

item.title: A century-old stellar mystery may finally have an explanation - Phys.org
item.description: A century-old stellar mystery may finally have an explanation  Phys.org

item.title: In 1980, Voyager 1 flew close enough to Titan to reveal its thick nitrogen atmosphere — but the gravity-assist geometry bent the spacecraft permanently out of the planetary plane, trading Uranus and Neptune for one close look at Saturn’s largest moon - Space Daily
item.description: In 1980, Voyager 1 flew close enough to Titan to reveal its thick nitrogen atmosphere — but the gravity-assist geometry bent the spacecraft permanently out of the planetary plane, trading Uranus and Neptune for one close look at Saturn’s largest moon  Space Daily

item.title: Geologists have taught for decades that rivers needed plants to form their distinctive S-shaped meanders, but Stanford University researchers published a study in August 2025 showing that rivers were carving those curves long before vegetation existed on la - Space Daily
item.description: Geologists have taught for decades that rivers needed plants to form their distinctive S-shaped meanders, but Stanford University researchers published a study in August 2025 showing that rivers were carving those curves long before vegetation existed on la  Space Daily

item.title: When elephants play, they rapidly copy one another’s trunk and head movements — a form of bodily mimicry resembling the contagious smiles and laughter seen in humans - Space Daily
item.description: When elephants play, they rapidly copy one another’s trunk and head movements — a form of bodily mimicry resembling the contagious smiles and laughter seen in humans  Space Daily

item.title: An AI searched nearly 100 million old Hubble images in two and a half days and found 1,300 cosmic oddities — more than 800 of them had never appeared in the scientific literature - Space Daily
item.description: An AI searched nearly 100 million old Hubble images in two and a half days and found 1,300 cosmic oddities — more than 800 of them had never appeared in the scientific literature  Space Daily

item.title: Mercury is shrinking — as its iron core slowly cools, the whole planet has contracted by up to seven kilometres, wrinkling its surface into cliffs hundreds of miles long - Space Daily
item.description: Mercury is shrinking — as its iron core slowly cools, the whole planet has contracted by up to seven kilometres, wrinkling its surface into cliffs hundreds of miles long  Space Daily

item.description:
1. Physicists Just Turned a Black Hole Energy Theory Into Reality  SciTechDaily

  3. 🌀 Physicists extract energy from a 'black hole' in the lab  Techno-Science
  4. Recreating the 'Stealing Energy from a Black Hole' Thought Experiment on a Tabletop: How an Electronic Circuit Overturned the Principles of Wave Amplification  XenoSpectrum
  5. Scientists recreate black hole energy effect in lab  Azərtac
